ref: Jour: Journal of Radioanalytical and Nuclear Chemistry, Vol.334, p.8261 (2025)
title: Measurement of thermal neutron capture cross section and resonance integral of the 64Ni(n,gamma)65Ni reaction using an electron LINAC based neutron source

x4freetext: Measurement of thermal neutron capture cross section
reaction using an electron LINAC based neutron source

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Journal of Radioanalytical and Nuclear Chemistry, Vol.334, p.8261 (2025)

x4codes.hlp: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (JINR), Dubna
Institute of Physics, VAST, Hanoi, Vietnam
Vietnam Atomic Energy Institute (VINATOM), Hanoi, Vietnam

x4freetext: Dalat University, 01 Phu Dong Thien Vuong,
Institute of Fundamental and Applied Sciences,
Duy Tan University, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam.

x4codes.hlp: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (JINR), Dubna

x4freetext: Electron beam of 110 MeV energy, 50 Hz
cycle repetition rate, 120 ns pulse width, average
Tungsten-based alloy with a density of
18.075 g cm.3, consisting of 90% W, 7% Ni, 3% Fe, was
employed as the target (4 cm diameter, 10 cm length).
Fast neutrons were thermalized by water inside
Al container of 16 cm diameter, 25 cm height, filled
Thermal neutron flux at the sample position -
Au sample size 10 x 10 x 0.05 mm, purity 99.98 %,
Ni sample size 10 x 10 x 0.3 mm, purity 99.5 %,
One set of Ni and Au samples was individually
covered with 0.5 mm thick Cd foils, while other set of
Ni and Au samples was uncovered. The samples were
irradiated simultaneously and continuously for 54000 s.
Shape factor of the epithermal neutron spectrum
using the cadmium-ratio dual monitor method.
Canberra HPGe detector GC4018 of 59 mm diameter,
61 mm height. Relative efficiency 40 % for 1332 keV
gamma line, energy resolution 0.875 keV at 122 keV and
1.8 keV at 1332 keV. Gamma detection efficiency was
determined by a set of standard sources of 57Co, 60Co,
Genie2000 software was used for the acquisition and
Neutron self-shielding, gamma self-absorption in the
sample, coincidence summing, geometrical, and dead-time
